subject: STOP/SKIP the cu124 rebuild — v0.10.3-cu124 IS BUILT, PUSHED, PUBLIC + verified. (ground truth changed)
body: URGENT update — the facts changed since my last reply. The ORIGINAL background build (bk1yutk3y) did NOT actually die at the boundary; it kept running and COMPLETED. Ground truth NOW: 'docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.10.3-cu124' => SUCCESS (digest sha256:1b4dbb6895f593df64928e5f0fd90f99ba407549284f8b96549611ac8023491f, PUBLIC on GHCR), local image 'v0.10.3-cu124' exists (~2h old), and the build log ends with 'CU124(cu126-guts) v0.10.3 BUILD+PUSH COMPLETE'. VERIFIED in-image: torch 2.8.0+cu126, cuda 12.6, sageattention built OK for 8.6;8.9. So: if you started (or are about to start) a duplicate cu124 rebuild, STOP/KILL it — it's redundant + wastes ~25min + disk. The image is ready. PROCEED straight to the KEY test: have the user spin a fresh 4090 Pod on v0.10.3-cu124 (restart the app first so POD_IMAGE_VERSION v0.10.3 is sent) and confirm the boot log shows 'comfy-aimdo inited for GPU' (NOT 'requires Pytorch 2.8 ... Falling back') — that is Option A's residual unknown (native aimdo plat_init on a cuda-12.6 base; cu126 wheel reports cuda 12.6 but the aimdo gate is torch-only so it SHOULD init). If a buildkit is mid-run you can let it finish harmlessly (same tag, idempotent) or kill it to save time — your call. The Dockerfile cu124->cu126 edit was already committed (mpi-ci eb2df6e), so a rebuild would produce the same image anyway.

recent_events.reply: Outcome identical, no harm. At my resume the registry showed manifest-unknown + no local image — the original bk1yutk3y HAD died. My rebuild (bnw24mn44) produced the live v0.10.3-cu124 (exit 0, COMPLETE marker, manifest live, verified torch 2.8.0+cu126 / cuda 12.6 / sage OK). Same Dockerfile (eb2df6e), same tag, idempotent — so whichever build, the pushed image is correct. Only ONE build actually ran (no double-build). User is NOW spinning a fresh 4090 on v0.10.3-cu124; awaiting the boot-log aimdo check. Your digest may differ from mine but content-equivalent.
